changes.value:
kanban-app/src/state.rs -- BoardHandle::open()

This is application code (Tauri), so `anyhow` is the recommended error type. Currently all errors are mapped to `String` via `format!("{e}")`, which discards the error chain. The Rust review guidelines require `.context()` on every `?` in app code.

Multiple `.map_err(|e| format!("...: {e}"))` calls lose the source error for debugging.

Suggestion: Switch the return type from `Result<Self, String>` to `anyhow::Result<Self>` and use `.context("what we were doing")` instead of `.map_err(format)`.
BoardHandle::open error handling uses .map_err(|e| format!()) -- loses error chain
